title: Reframe the target as discrete-time survival on remaining stint length
summary: This is naturally a time-to-event problem: each row asks whether the next event is a pit on the following lap.
rationale: This is naturally a time-to-event problem: each row asks whether the next event is a pit on the following lap. Survival models are designed for event timing with censoring, which matches rows that never pit again before race end.
implementation_hint: Create a stint-end label per row: time-until-next-pit in laps, right-censor rows with no future pit before race end, then train a discrete hazard model (`LogisticHazard`/MTLR) or XGBoost `survival:aft`. Convert predictions back to one-step hazard `P(pit at t+1 | alive at t)` for submission or blending.
expected_effect: Often improves temporal consistency and calibration over independent row-wise binary classification, while using the same base covariates plus lags.
risk: More plumbing and harder validation; a survival model can underperform if the censoring setup is wrong or if the competition metric rewards only local discrimination.
